Here is the natural language question for generating SQL:
Retrieve the top 5 posts based on the number of likes, among users who created the post's category. -- Include the post id and the count of number of likes
sanitized_sql:
SELECT
T1.post_id,
T1.likes_count
FROM tbl_posts AS T1
INNER JOIN tbl_categories AS T2
ON T1.category_id = T2.category_id
WHERE
T1.user_id = T2.created_by
ORDER BY
T1.likes_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

Show average comment length by browser for users who exclusively use a specific browser
generated_sql: SELECT T1.browser, AVG(LENGTH(T2.content)) FROM (SELECT user_id, browser FROM tbl_user_activity GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT browser) = 1) AS T1 INNER JOIN tbl_comments AS T2 ON T1.user_id = T2.user_id GROUP BY T1.browser
golden_sql: ['WITH users_exclusive AS (SELECT user_id FROM tbl_user_activity GROUP BY user_id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT browser) = 1), users_browsers AS (SELECT user_id, browser FROM tbl_user_activity WHERE user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM users_exclusive)) SELECT browser, AVG(LENGTH(content)) FROM tbl_comments c INNER JOIN users_browsers u ON c.user_id = u.user_id GROUP BY browser;']

Here is the natural language question for generating SQL:
Which posts have tags related to both 'programming' and 'database' ?
generated_sql:
SELECT
T1.title
FROM tbl_posts AS T1
INNER JOIN tbl_posts_tags AS T2
ON T1.post_id = T2.post_id
INNER JOIN tbl_tags AS T3
ON T2.tag_id = T3.tag_id
WHERE
T3.name IN ('programming', 'database')
GROUP BY
T1.post_id
HAVING
COUNT(T3.tag_id) = 2;

T1.title
FROM tbl_posts AS T1
INNER JOIN tbl_posts_tags AS T2
ON T1.post_id = T2.post_id
INNER JOIN tbl_tags AS T3

GROUP BY
T1.post_id

COUNT(T3.tag_id) = 2;

Number of messages per day for conversations that span multiple days and have no read messages.
generated_sql: SELECT DATE(T1.sending_time), COUNT(*) FROM tbl_private_messages AS T1 INNER JOIN (SELECT conversation_id FROM tbl_private_messages GROUP BY conversation_id HAVING SUM(is_read) = 0 AND MAX(DATE(sending_time)) > MIN(DATE(sending_time))) AS T2 ON T1.conversation_id = T2.conversation_id GROUP BY DATE(T1.sending_time)
golden_sql: ['WITH conversations AS (SELECT conversation_id, (julianday(MAX(sending_time)) - julianday(MIN(sending_time))) * 86400 AS conv_length FROM tbl_private_messages WHERE conversation_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY conversation_id HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN is_read THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) = 0) SELECT pm.conversation_id, DATE(pm.sending_time), COUNT(*) FROM tbl_private_messages pm INNER JOIN conversations c ON pm.conversation_id = c.conversation_id WHERE c.conv_length > 86400 GROUP BY pm.conversation_id, DATE(pm.sending_time);']
